name: xargs
description: Execute a command with whitespace-delimited strings (from stdin) as arguments

options.description: Use NUL (0x00) as a separator, instead of whitespace

options.name: -E
options.description: Use this string as a logical EOF marker

args.name: eof-str
args.description: The string to use that marks EOF

options.name: -I
options.description: Replace occurrences of this string with the input

options.name: -J
options.description: Replace an argument exactly equal to this string with the input

options.name: -L
options.description: Run the program each time this many lines of input are read

options.name: -n
options.description: The maximum number of arguments that can be taken from stdin on each run

options.name: -o
options.description: Reopen stdin as /dev/tty (useful for running interactive applications)

options.name: -P
options.description: Run up to this many commands in parallel (as many as possible if 0)

options.name: -r
options.description: Run the command once if there's no input (compatible with GNU xargs)

options.name: -R
options.description: Specify the maximum number of occurrences that -I will replace

options.name: -S
options.description: Specify the maximum size in bytes that -I can use for replacements (default: 255)

options.name: -s
options.description: Maximum number of bytes that can be provided to the program (default: 4096)

options.name: -t
options.description: Echo the command to stderr before it's executed

options.name: -x
options.description: Terminal if the arguments will not fit in the maximum line length
